title: Lippi, Madonna and Child with two Angels (quiz)
topic_path: Lippi, Madonna and Child with two Angels (quiz)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Lippi's *Madonna and Child with two Angels* is different from previous representations of the same subject because it reveals a more [[☃ dropdown 1]] approach by the artist. This is especially seen in the [[☃ dropdown 2]] of the figures.**

![Painting of the Virgin Mary in prayer, seated on a carved chair with the Christ Child on her lap and an angel beside them, set before a window that opens to a detailed landscape, creating a strong sense of spatial depth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb61673f6e2b86cdd79b793b30cfa07c.jpg)
hints:
**This painting shows the influence of Renaissance Humanism in the light tone of the scene.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/early-renaissance1/painting-in-Florence/v/fra-filippo-lippi-madonna-and-child-with-two-angels-c-1460-1465)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Lippi's Mary appears real and beautiful, which departs from earlier representations of the Madonna and Child.**


![Detail of Lippi’s Madonna with a lifelike face, delicate veil, and graceful beauty, set before a distant landscape.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f6c435ca0e552a8c188870b439f8847e.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Lippi departed from the more distant representations of Mary that were popular in the early fifteenth century.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/early-renaissance1/painting-in-Florence/v/fra-filippo-lippi-madonna-and-child-with-two-angels-c-1460-1465)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fra Filippo Lippi used [[☃ dropdown 1]] to create a real landscape.**



![Detail of a painted landscape with distant hills fading into softer, bluish tones to suggest depth and distance.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4988e1cf704948747f3eb48acc6387cd.jpg)

**Which of the following is NOT true of this painting?**

![Painting of the Virgin Mary in prayer, seated on a carved chair with the Christ Child on her lap and an angel beside them, set before a window that opens to a detailed landscape, creating a strong sense of spatial depth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb61673f6e2b86cdd79b793b30cfa07c.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Fra Filippo Lippi used decorative details, beauty, and Humanism to bring the divine subjects into an earthly space.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/renaissance-reformation/early-renaissance1/painting-in-Florence/v/fra-filippo-lippi-madonna-and-child-with-two-angels-c-1460-1465)**

choices.content: Lippi was interested in the decorative elements of the painting.
Lippi tried to create a sense of beauty in the figure of Mary.
The painting has an earthy tone that reflects the ideas of Renaissance Humanism.
Lippi emphasized the golden divine light of the scene, creating distance between the figures and the viewer.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fra Filippo Lippi's *Madonna and Child with two Angels* demonstrates a shift toward more naturalistic depictions of religious subjects in the Renaissance.   Match the description to the appropriate image and organize them in chronological order.  **

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints:
**While this painting suggests some spatial depth in the treatment of the architecture, the symmetry and linear patterning recalls Byzantine traditions, and helps identify it as the earliest of these examples.  **

[[☃ image 1]]

**This painting shows the use of modeling to create three dimensional forms, but the treatment of the background reflects medieval traditions to distinguish the spiritual space occupied by religious figures.**

[[☃ image 1]]

The naturalistic poses, subtle modeling of the figures, and atmospheric perspective used to depict the background landscape shows the shift toward more humanistic portrayals of the Madonna and child in the Renaissance.  

[[☃ image 1]]

left: ![Virgin Mary seated with the Christ Child, surrounded by angels and prophets below, with overlapping figures and a flat gold background that shows limited spatial depth compared to later works.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5b0f29d1fcb93f827db60a89f87d900b.jpg)

![Virgin Mary seated on a Gothic throne holding the Christ Child, surrounded by angels, with the throne rendered in perspective to suggest spatial depth and three-dimensionality.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/11242525e91bcc38cad1380666c82bcd.jpg)

![Painting of the Virgin Mary in prayer, seated on a carved chair with the Christ Child on her lap and an angel beside them, set before a window that opens to a detailed landscape, creating a strong sense of spatial depth.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d0013e3679431efa5f248eac01339184.jpg)
right: While this painting suggests some spatial depth in the treatment of the architecture, the symmetry and linear patterning recalls Byzantine traditions, and helps identify it as the earliest of these examples.

This painting shows the use of modeling to create three dimensional forms, but the treatment of the background reflects medieval traditions to distinguish the spiritual space occupied by religious figures.

The naturalistic poses, subtle modeling of the figures, and atmospheric perspective used to depict the background landscape shows the shift toward more humanistic portrayals of the Madonna and child in the Renaissance.
